dataset: WHO SAGE Vaccine Hesitancy Determinants Framework
case_id: Vaccine Acceptance and Hesitancy Study (LimeSurvey survey)

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 63 - survey created with api('add_survey', [session, 0, 'Vaccine Acceptance and Hesitancy Study', 'en', 'G'])
ISO 639-1 standard for Spanish; verified in export_result.sh line 46 checking for 'es' language code
8 questions total (Q1-Q8 created in setup_task.sh lines 85-159); verifier.py lines 115-119 confirm >= 6 questions needed for full credit. 6 represents 75% translation completeness.

Spanish language keyword - 'vacun' is prefix found in 'vacuna' (vaccine), 'vacunación' (vaccination), 'vacunas' (vaccines). Verified in verifier.py line 86 with pattern list and export_result.sh line 50 checking for 'vacun|encuesta|aceptaci|hesitaci|vacuna'

pi_items.metadata_value: Survey Researchers / Social Science Research Assistants
pi_items.verified_value: SOC 19-3022 (Survey Researchers) / SOC 19-4061 (Social Science Research Assistants)
pi_items.source: master_dataset.csv contains both SOC codes. Verified via: (1) US BLS occupational database showing SOC 19-3022.00 Survey Researchers; (2) US BLS occupational database showing SOC 19-4061.00 Social Science Research Assistants; (3) O*NET OnLine databases confirming both occupations exist

pi_items.metadata_value: WHO SAGE Working Group on Vaccine Hesitancy determinants framework
pi_items.verified_value: WHO SAGE Working Group on Vaccine Hesitancy (2012-2014) Determinants Framework; Larson et al. 2014 systematic review in Vaccine journal
pi_items.source: Web search and WHO official sources confirm: (1) WHO SAGE WG on Vaccine Hesitancy was active March 2012-November 2014; (2) Larson et al. 2014 published 'Vaccine hesitancy: Definition, scope and determinants' in Vaccine journal, indexed in PubMed; (3) Framework published October 2014 with determinants: Confidence (safety/efficacy/trust), Complacency (perceived risk), Convenience (access/affordability); (4) Survey questions in setup_task.sh directly address these determinants (Q5-Importance/Complacency, Q6-Safety/Confidence, Q7-Effectiveness/Confidence, Q3-Access/Convenience)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real survey instrument based on the WHO SAGE Working Group on Vaccine Hesitancy determinants framework (Larson et al., 2014, Vaccine journal). The survey titled 'Vaccine Acceptance and Hesitancy Study' contains 8 questions covering key vaccine hesitancy determinants: vaccination status, vaccine types, access/convenience, side effects, perceived importance/complacency, safety confidence, effectiveness confidence, and hesitancy reasons. The task requires translating this survey into Spanish (es language code), with minimum 6 of 8 questions translated and Spanish title containing vaccine-related keyword 'vacun'. The target occupations (Survey Researchers SOC 19-3022 and Social Science Research Assistants SOC 19-4061) are authentic US Bureau of Labor Statistics occupation codes representing roles that regularly conduct multilingual surveys in public health research.
